parsed_review.summary: No boundary drift. All factories/validators are deterministic descriptor builders; no DB writes, no runtime permission eval, no audit append, no Hermes/Claude execution, no API handler, no UI, no network, synthetic-only data (example.invalid), no LDIP, no HRX split. Ownership boundaries (PermissionKernel/AuditKernel/MatterCore/DMS as reference-only) are declarative and respected.

findings.title: no_write_attestation validation is incomplete (defeated completeness guard)
findings.evidence: scripts/validate-rp04-master-data-contract.mjs loops the 19-key MASTER_DATA_CP156_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION but guards each check with `&& key in contract.no_write_attestation`. Any attestation key omitted from contracts/master-data-contract.json (which currently declares only 15) passes silently, and the 4 code-only keys (emits_hermes_evidence, sends_claude_prompt, mutates_dom, issues_network_request) are never required in the contract. A future contract declaring only a subset of false attestations would still validate.
findings.recommended_action: Require every registry attestation key to be present AND false in the contract; drop the `key in` guard and add a missing-key error so the core no-write boundary cannot drift silently.

findings.title: ownership_drift acceptance risk has zero automated coverage
findings.evidence: validators.js implements the ownership_drift block (`options.owner_module !== definition.owner_module`), but neither packages/master-data/test/model.test.js nor scripts/validate-rp04-master-data-contract.mjs exercises it. It is the only one of the five acceptance risks with no passing/failing assertion. The 'owner drift must block' criterion is therefore unverified.
findings.recommended_action: Add a test asserting validateMasterDataRecord(..., { owner_module: 'DMS' }) returns valid:false with blocked_claims including 'ownership_drift', mirroring the leakage/direction tests.

findings.title: Relationship direction validation in validateMasterDataRecord is narrow
findings.evidence: validators.js only flags `from_entity_id === to_entity_id` (self-loop). It does not validate the `direction` enum nor that direction is consistent with endpoint entity kinds; the enum is only enforced in the model.js factory, so a record built outside the factory with an invalid/mismatched direction passes validation. Also, when both endpoints are absent, `undefined === undefined` yields a false-positive relationship_direction_error alongside missing_required_fields.
findings.recommended_action: In the validator, reject direction values not in MASTER_DATA_RELATIONSHIP_DIRECTIONS, and only run the self-loop check when both endpoints are present; document that direction/kind cross-consistency is deferred (no kind lookup available in descriptor-only scope).

findings.title: hidden_source_fields absence is structurally guaranteed but untested
findings.evidence: Factories construct records via an explicit allowlist (baseRecord + per-model fields), so MASTER_DATA_CP156_HIDDEN_SOURCE_FIELDS (raw_document_body, secret, etc.) cannot leak through factory output. No test asserts this — e.g., passing { raw_document_body, secret } to a factory and verifying they are absent from the frozen record.
findings.recommended_action: Add one regression test confirming hidden source fields supplied as input never appear on the returned record.

findings.title: contract.public_exports under-declares the real surface and is unvalidated
findings.evidence: contracts/master-data-contract.json lists 11 exports, but src/index.js re-exports all of model/registry/validators (e.g., createMasterDataEntity, MASTER_DATA_LIFECYCLE_STATUSES, getMasterDataModelDefinition, missingRequiredFields, createMasterDataCp156CoverageSummary). The validation script never checks public_exports, so this list can drift from the actual API without detection.
findings.recommended_action: Either validate public_exports against the package's actual named exports or relabel the field as an illustrative subset.

findings.title: ContactPoint omits owner_user_id from required_fields, unlike all other models
findings.evidence: registry.js ContactPoint.required_fields = [contact_point_id, tenant_id, owner_entity_id, contact_type, value, status] — no owner_user_id, while Entity/Person/Organization/ClientGroup/Relationship/BillingProfile all require it. Likely intentional (owned via owner_entity_id), but the inconsistency is undocumented.
findings.recommended_action: Confirm intent and add a brief note in the registry/README, or align required_fields if user-ownership is expected on contact points.

findings.title: package-lock adds an unrelated control-plane workspace entry
findings.evidence: package-lock.json registers both @law-firm-os/master-data and @law-firm-os/control-plane links/workspaces; only master-data is in scope for CP00-156. Appears to be benign lockfile reconciliation for a previously-unlocked workspace, but it is outside the pack's stated file set.
findings.recommended_action: Verify the control-plane lock entry is expected reconciliation (not an accidental include); no action if intentional.

parsed_review.p3_disposition: two fixed and two accepted as non-blocking workspace/descriptor-scope residuals
